signals.headline: South Korea's March 2026 Inflation Rises to 2
signals.raw:
South Korea's March 2026 Inflation Rises to 2.2%, Exceeding Central Bank Target | YES | HIGH confidence**

South Korea's **annual inflation rate rose to 2.2% in March 2026**, up from 2.0% in the previous two months but falling short of market forecasts of 2.4%[1]. This marks a significant development as inflation has moved above the Bank of Korea's 2% target, driven largely by **surging oil costs*

signals.headline: Market-moving implications:** Import prices in South Korea surged in March, marking their sharpest jump in almost three
signals.raw: Market-moving implications:** Import prices in South Korea surged in March, marking their sharpest jump in almost three decades[5], adding upside inflation pressure. The weak Korean won (rising to around 1,520 won per U.S. dollar) is amplifying import costs for energy and food[4].
